title: Grab a huge $550 saving on this 4K-ready gaming PC with an RTX 5070 and 7800X3D right now just $1,449 for this liquid-cooled iBuyPower rig with 16GB DDR5 and a 1TB SSD
summary: Grab a huge $550 saving on this 4K-ready gaming PC, featuring an AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D, NVIDIA RTX 5070, 16GB DDR5 RAM, and a 1TB SSD, all for just $1,449.
insight: Significant discount on high-end gaming PC with top specs.

title: Nashville Zoo pushes back on 1.6-acre data center build near animal habitats Zoo says it planned to use lot for education and conservation center
summary: Nashville Zoo opposes a 1.6-acre data center build near animal habitats, citing potential disruption and harm to the animals.
insight: Data centers near wildlife habitats pose risks to animal health and well-being

title: Linux developers are using AI vibe coding to keep vintage AMD GPUs alive R600 driver cleaned up with GitHub Copilot gives HD 2000 to HD 6000 series a new lease of life
summary: Linux developers leverage AI to revive support for vintage AMD GPUs with the R600 driver update, giving a new lease of life to HD 2000 to HD 6000 series graphics cards.
insight: Reviving vintage GPUs extends their lifespan and reduces electronic waste.

title: Levelplay shows off magnetically attached fans that are reversible, connect via pogo pins and USB-C plus an AIO that trades a screen for a big knob
summary: Levelplay showcased innovative cooling solutions, including reversibly attached fans and a unique AIO design with a tactile knob for fan control.
insight: Reimagining cooling systems for improved usability and customization.

title: Chinese startup claims photonic chip production without DUV lithography, says nanoimprint process cuts costs by 90% 8-inch wafers produced without conventional optical lithography
summary: Chinese startup Prinano achieves photonic chip production without DUV lithography using nanoimprint technology, reducing costs by 90%
insight: Revolutionary nanoimprint tech slashes photonic chip production costs dramatically worldwide

title: Apple demonstrates cross-platform Siri upgrades in macOS 27 Golden Gate at WWDC update brings Liquid Glass improvements and unifies AI strategy
summary: Apple's macOS 27 Golden Gate update introduces refined Liquid Glass design and unified AI features, including cross-platform Siri upgrades.
insight: Unifying AI strategy enhances user experience across Apple devices.

title: Intel expands new game-boosting iBOT software with seven more games, up to a 27% improvement Team Blue claims 12% average jump in newly-supported titles
summary: Intel expands its iBOT feature, adding seven new games for improved performance.
insight: Enhanced gaming experience with significant performance boosts

title: Most new U.S. AI data centers are being built in drought zones two-thirds of 809 planned projects set for areas with water shortages
summary: Two-thirds of planned US data centers are built in drought zones, exacerbating water shortages. This poses significant challenges for cooling and water management. The trend may have long-term environmental implications.
insight: Drought-stricken areas struggle to support water-intensive AI infrastructure needs.

title: Farmer donates land for a park, city sells it for data center development $10 gift became $10M for city government, with $30M tax expected over next decade
summary: Texas farmland donated in 1999 for a public park was sold to a data center developer for $10 million, with the city expecting a $30 million tax revenue over the next decade.
insight: City's land use policy shift generates significant tax revenue.

title: Demand for data center CPUs has surged, and AI agents are responsible why the CPU to GPU ratio is more important than ever for hyperscalers
summary: The AI gold rush is driving a surge in demand for data center CPUs, causing a bottleneck. Industry experts attribute this to the increasing use of AI workloads and the need for more powerful processing capabilities. Hyperscalers must now focus on optimizing their CPU to GPU ratios to meet the growing demand.
insight: Optimizing CPU to GPU ratios is crucial for AI workload efficiency.

title: Disgruntled ASML employees threaten to boycott Elon Musk conference appearance staff express ire at political involvement and 'Nazi sympathies'
summary: ASML employees protest Elon Musk's invitation to their annual tech conference, citing his political involvement and alleged 'Nazi sympathies'. The employees threaten a boycott in response. The situation has sparked internal debate at the equipment maker.
insight: Employees rebel against Elon Musk's appearance over controversial views and politics

title: Travlfi JourneyGo 5G mobile hotspot review Affordably priced, but lacking in performance and features
summary: The Travlfi JourneyGo 5G offers a budget-friendly option, but lacks performance and features.
insight: Affordability comes at the cost of performance and functionality.

title: Altera Agilex 9 Direct RF-Series AGRW039 SoC FPGA targets high-performance RF systems
summary: The Altera Agilex 9 Direct RF-Series AGRW039 SoC FPGA is designed for high-performance Radio Frequency (RF) systems, offering increased logic, DSP resources, and a wideband RF. It integrates a quad-core Cortex-A53 processor and supports DDR5 and LPDDR5 memory. This SoC FPGA is suitable for aerospace, defense, and advanced communications systems.
insight: Enables more efficient and powerful RF systems with increased processing capabilities.

title: GEEKOM A7 2026 Edition (AMD Ryzen 5 7545U) mini PC review Part 1: specifications, unboxing, teardown, and first boot
summary: GEEKOM A7 2026 Edition mini PC review covers specifications, unboxing, teardown, and first boot on Windows 11 Pro.
insight: Comparing affordable 2026 Edition to its pricier Ryzen 9 7940HS sibling matters.

title: Best of Sensors: Woman of the Year Tianyue Yu
summary: Tianyue Yu was recognized for her work as a technologist and founder with over two decades of experience.
insight: Pioneering female tech leaders drive innovation and industry growth.

title: A thief used a Waymo as a getaway car. Six months later, police still have no suspect
summary: A burglar used a Waymo robotaxi as a getaway vehicle after breaking into a yoga studio in San Francisco's Marina District. The thief entered, grabbed merchandise, and left within minutes. Six months later, no suspect has been identified.
insight: Unidentified suspect raises concerns about autonomous vehicle security and surveillance.

title: PepsiCo deploys fleet of driverless trucks to deliver Doritos and drinks in three states
summary: PepsiCo deploys driverless trucks to deliver products across three states, serving Walmart and Dollar General.
insight: Automation transforms logistics, increasing efficiency and reducing transportation costs for large companies.

title: Apple iOS 27 brings long-awaited Siri AI overhaul, with daily usage limits unless you pay for iCloud+
summary: iOS 27 brings generative AI features and strengthens child-safety controls to Apple devices.
insight: Siri AI overhaul raises concerns about usage limits without iCloud+ subscription.

title: AV2 promises 30% better compression than AV1 and costs nothing to use
summary: AV2, a new video codec, offers 30% better compression than AV1 at no additional cost.
insight: Higher compression efficiency at no extra cost boosts industry adoption.

title: Happy Birthday, Intel 8086: World's first x86 processor debuted exactly 48 years ago today
summary: The Intel 8086, a world's first x86 processor, debuted exactly 48 years ago. It was initially conceived as a stopgap due to delays in the iAPX 432. The 8086 was developed in just 18 months and supported demanding applications.
insight: Pivotal shift in microprocessors led to widespread computational innovation.

title: Thief: The Dark Project remaster brings 1998 stealth classic to modern hardware
summary: Nightdive Studios is remastering Thief: The Dark Project for modern hardware, preserving the original experience while updating visuals.
insight: Classic stealth gameplay returns to modern platforms with updated graphics.

title: EV drivers are showing up to public chargers and finding locked gates and surprise fees
summary: Public EV chargers are often locked, restricted, or overpriced, causing frustration for new EV drivers. This can create a negative experience, making it harder for people to adopt electric vehicles. The issue is exacerbated by inconsistent charging network standards.
insight: Unpredictable charging experiences hinder EV adoption and user satisfaction.

title: US microreactor achieves first self-sustaining nuclear chain reaction, targets 2028 deployment
summary: US microreactor achieves a self-sustaining nuclear chain reaction, marking a historic milestone.
insight: Advances nuclear energy, potentially increasing carbon-free electricity production capacity.

title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope is launching August 30, eight months ahead of schedule
summary: NASA's Roman Space Telescope is launching ahead of schedule on August 30. After arrival, it will undergo a post-travel inspection. This telescope will then begin its mission.
insight: Early launch could accelerate discoveries in space and astronomy research.

title: US government could take a stake in OpenAI as Trump explores AI equity plan
summary: US government officials have discussed receiving shares in major AI firms, including OpenAI. This move is part of a potential AI equity plan being explored. The plan's details and implications are still unclear.
insight: Government equity in AI firms could influence tech industry direction

title: AMD RDNA 5 and Nvidia Rubin GPUs could both slip to late 2027 or 2028, board partners suggest
summary: AMD RDNA 5 and Nvidia Rubin GPUs may be delayed until 2027 or 2028, according to board partners, with some expecting mid-2027 releases and others anticipating later availability. An announcement and actual retail launch may not align. This delay could impact the gaming market and related industries.
insight: Potential GPU delay impacts gaming industry momentum and sales.
